explanation: This LTL property states that whenever the pilot is not in control (!standby) and the system is supported without failures (!apfail), and the sensor limits are exceeded (limits), then eventually within N_DURATION time units the autopilot will pullup. The boolean expression bool_exp2 represents the condition of pilot not being in control and system being supported without failures (!standby & !apfail). The boolean expression bool_exp3 represents the sensor limits being exceeded (limits). The LTL template chosen ensures that the pullup happens within N_DURATION time units of when the condition is met.

This LTL property states that if the system is supported and not in autopilot failure (!apfail & supported), and the sensor limits are exceeded (limits), then the autopilot will eventually enter the pullup state (pullup).  The G operator ensures this holds globally, meaning at any point in time. The F operator means that the pullup condition will eventually be true after the limits condition has been met.  The "pilot is not in control" is implicitly handled by the fact that the system needs to be supported to proceed.

This LTL formula states that whenever the system is supported and the pilot is not in standby, and the sensor limits are exceeded, then the autopilot pullup is activated within N_DURATION time units.  It uses template 14 to express this implication: if bool_exp2 (supported & !standby & limits) is true, then eventually within N_DURATION time units, bool_exp3 (pullup) will be true. The choice of template 14 is appropriate because it captures the response behavior of the system within a bounded time frame.  Other templates are unsuitable because they do not capture this response relationship within a specific time window.

This LTL property states that whenever the sensor limits are exceeded (!limits), and the pilot is not in control (!standby), and the system is supported (supported) and there is no autopilot failure (!apfail), then it must eventually lead to a pullup within N_DURATION time units. It uses template 24 because it expresses a response property in which after a certain condition is met, the system eventually responds in a way, which is specified in the boolean expression.

This LTL property states that if the sensor limits are exceeded (!limits) and the pilot is not in control (!standby) and the system is supported (supported) and there is no autopilot failure (!apfail), then eventually within N_DURATION time units the autopilot will pull up (pullup).  The G operator ensures that this property holds globally, that is at all timesteps. The F operator states that eventually (at least one point in the future) the pullup state will be true.

This LTL property states that if the sensor limits are exceeded (!limits) and the pilot is not in control (!standby) and the system is supported (supported) and there are no autopilot failures (!apfail), then the autopilot shall eventually enter the pullup state (F pullup). The G operator ensures this is true globally. The index of the chosen LTL template is 19. N_DURATION is set to 1.

This LTL specification states that Globally, if the system is supported and not in standby and sensor limits are exceeded, then it is guaranteed that within N_DURATION time units the autopilot will transition to the pullup state.  The boolean expressions ensure that the system's health and pilot control status are correctly accounted for.

This LTL formula states that whenever the system is supported (!apfail) and the pilot is not in control (!standby) and the sensor limits are exceeded (limits), then eventually within N_DURATION time units the autopilot will pullup.  The chosen template accounts for the reactive nature of the requirement, and also allows for the pullup action to occur after a delay of up to N_DURATION time units (allowing for system delays and transitions).

This LTL property states that if the system is supported and not in standby, and the sensor limits are exceeded, then the autopilot will eventually enter pullup mode within N_DURATION time units.  The boolean expressions correctly represent the natural language conditions and the overall LTL structure uses the implication operator to capture causality between the sensor exceeding limits and the autopilot entering the pullup mode.

This LTL property states that if the system is supported and not in autopilot failure (!apfail & supported), and if the sensor limits are exceeded (limits), then the autopilot pullup maneuver will be activated eventually within N_DURATION time units.  The property uses the implication operator (->) to link the condition of a supported system and exceeded sensor limits to the eventual activation of the autopilot pullup, which is represented by pullup.  The G operator ensures that this implication holds globally (at every time instant). The F operator indicates that the event of pullup will eventually happen in the future, within N_DURATION time units. The negation of standby (!standby) ensures that this behavior is only considered when the pilot is not in control, and the system is healthy.

This LTL formula states that if the system is supported and the pilot is not in control, and the sensor limits are exceeded, then the autopilot will eventually transition to the pullup state within N_DURATION time units.  The formula uses implication (->) to express the conditional relationship between the sensor limits being exceeded and the autopilot transitioning to the pullup state. The atomic proposition supported ensures that the system is healthy. The atomic proposition !standby indicates that the pilot is not in control. The atomic proposition limits indicates the sensor limit has been exceeded. The atomic proposition pullup indicates that the autopilot is in the pullup state. The temporal operator F[0, N_DURATION] ensures that the autopilot will transition to the pullup state within N_DURATION time units.

This LTL specification asserts that whenever the system is supported and the pilot is not in control (not standby), and the sensor limits are exceeded, then the autopilot will eventually transition to the pullup state within N_DURATION time units.  It uses template 14 to express this. bool_exp2 represents the condition of the system being supported and the pilot not being in control, and bool_exp3 represents the condition of the sensor limits being exceeded and the autopilot being in the pullup state. The implication (->) ensures that if bool_exp2 is true, then eventually bool_exp3 will become true within N_DURATION time units.

This LTL formula checks that whenever the sensor limits are exceeded (!limits), and the pilot is not in control (!standby) and the system is supported (supported), then eventually within N_DURATION time units the autopilot will pull up (pullup). The chosen template is suitable because it expresses a response property, which fits the 'shall latch' requirement.  The antecedent combines the conditions of exceeding limits and pilot not in control; the consequent specifies the pull-up response, eventually within a bounded time frame.

This LTL property states that if the sensor limits are exceeded (!limits), and the pilot is not in control (!standby) and the system is supported (supported) without failures (!apfail), then eventually within N_DURATION time units, the autopilot will pull up (pullup).  The use of G (always) ensures that this condition holds for all times. The choice of template accounts for the fact that the pullup action is expected to occur sometime after the condition is detected, as opposed to immediately in the next step.

It uses the template 14 to express this.

The use of G (always) ensures the implication always holds, and F (eventually) specifies that pullup will occur within the specified timeframe.
